examples: He got up very sulkily and crossed over to the other side of the court.
The Dormouse sulkily remarked, "If you can't be civil, you'd better finish the story for yourself."
Is it worth keeping? 'I inquired, less sulkily.
For all these reasons, even if Russia turns sulkily inwards, it must be kept as strategically engaged with the West as possible.
Having been talked up last month, the putative negotiations, ostensibly aimed at stabilising Iraq, seem distant now that both sides have sulkily averred that they never really wanted to talk at all.
